summary:
The analysis indicates that this particular type of force likely results in reducing overbite due to its typically compressive nature influencing dental alignment more closely together versus separating elements farther away from their original positions during normal physiological functions such as biting down firmly with closed mandible onto maxillary arches.
The chosen option aligns best because most commonly encountered orthodontic cases show how direct downward pressure brings upper front teeth nearer lower ones thereby decreasing overall vertical gap measurements indicative characteristic changes seen when treating patients presenting initial signs suggesting excessive protrusion issues needing correction via corrective appliances designed
